page.title: Small interactive builds that make technical ideas more hands-on.
page.description: This page is a home for browser-based experiments and mini-games. It stays outside the main navigation so it can grow over time without cluttering the core portfolio.

section.heading: Current games and experiments
section.description: Each game is self-contained and playable in the browser. More will be added over time.

items.title: Dodgeball
items.label: Playable
items.imageAlt: Preview showing two paddles and a ball in flight across a divided arena
items.summary: A two-player game on one keyboard. Throw balls at your opponent and dodge the ones they throw back. First to lose all health loses.
items.note: A simplified browser version of the first game I ever programmed, originally built in Lua with drawn characters and animations.

items.title: RGB Guesser
items.label: Playable
items.imageAlt: Preview illustration for the RGB Guesser web game
items.summary: You are shown a target RGB value and have to choose the matching colour from a small set of swatches.
items.note: Useful for getting faster at reading RGB values and mentally translating them into visible colour.

items.title: Outlier Hunt
items.label: Playable
items.imageAlt: Preview showing two clusters of blue dots with one orange outlier clearly isolated in the corner
items.summary: Points are arranged into clusters. One or two do not belong. Click the outliers before the timer runs out. Wrong clicks cost three seconds.
items.note: Identifying points that sit outside every cluster is a real task in spatial analysis and anomaly detection. This game makes that pattern recognition feel immediate.
